release_review.summary: Redeven v0.10.0 consumes published Floret v0.12.0, Flowersec v0.27.0, and Floe Webapp v0.38.0 while preserving Floret authority and the existing Runtime Service wire contract.
release_review.same_window_rationale: The project has no deployed older Runtime or Desktop population. Only the current ai_threadstore_product_v2 schema v2 is migrated to v3; unsupported kinds and versions are rejected without modification. The Runtime Service wire protocol remains redeven-runtime-v1.
checked_surfaces: Flower production hosts explicitly select Floret provider-owned thread titles; manual names, create-time explicit titles, migrations, and fork titles use Floret SetThreadTitle without a Redeven title shadow or retry worker

Provider adapters map Floret typed messages directly to provider wire shapes and reject invalid roles, ordering, tool arguments, tool results, and provider state instead of grouping, repairing, deduplicating, dropping, or synthesizing data

Floret live ThreadTurnProjection values remain running until a terminal marker, allowing tool-call and dispatch activity to reach Flower through the existing message.block_set event before tool completion

Redeven delegates event, stream, activity timeline, projection, availability, context snapshot, compaction result, latest-turn, control signal, approval, todo, attachment, and pending-tool settlement validation to Floret v0.12.0 public contracts and adds only product identity checks and UI mapping

Current thread permission is read from Redeven settings before every provider step and local dispatch; missing, invalid, unreadable, empty, or unpersistable snapshot state stops execution without a stale-snapshot or approval_required fallback

Current queued attachments are validated for exact queue ownership, metadata, file bytes, size, MIME, model capability, and provider route before Floret admission; canonical provider projection resolves only thread-owned opaque resources and never degrades to filename text

One Floret SQLite Store is opened per AI Service and shared by ordinary turns, idle compaction, subagents, and maintenance hosts; those facades do not close it, and Service.Close releases running resources before closing the Store once

Opaque provider continuation is persisted only by Floret and keyed by journal leaf plus a non-sensitive compatibility key derived from provider id/type, normalized endpoint, wire model, and transport route; Redeven stores only credentials and product profile preferences

Live context events update only current-process Flower state, while bootstrap and reconnect read the canonical validated ThreadContextSnapshot through Floret ReadThreadContext without a Redeven context cursor, event mirror, or mapped database copy

A pending terminal process binds its complete PendingToolSettlementTarget and creating Floret Host before launch; top-level and subagent processes settle through that owner without run registry lookup, Host guessing, or ThreadMaintenanceHost fallback

A successful terminal.terminate stops the Redeven PTY, waits for process-tree reap and output drain, settles the original pending terminal.exec once through the bound Floret Host, writes product operation audit, and applies the canonical Floret projection without a second provider request or pkill/kill compensation
terminal.read and terminal.write are PTY-only operations and cannot trigger, wait for, or retry Floret settlement; Service.Close completes process finalizers before releasing Floret Hosts and stores
Public Floret EventSink traffic contains only observation event types; thread_started, thread_resumed, entry_appended, and other private harness lifecycle events remain on HarnessSink

Flower live bootstrap, paged history, and timeline.replaced use the same strict timeline decoration union; unknown kinds, missing payloads, mixed payloads, and invalid anchors fail explicitly instead of being filtered or repaired

Terminal turns with missing, invalid, or non-renderable saved projections produce one turn_projection_unavailable entry after the matching user message, while active turns remain undecorated and no empty assistant message or retry action is synthesized

Thread deletion persists an immutable user-intent and upload cleanup snapshot before external effects, deletes the canonical Floret thread first, transactionally removes Redeven settings and ownership, then retries read-state and physical file cleanup until every confirmation is durable

Thread deletion restart coverage includes crashes after product commit, file cleanup confirmation, Floret deletion confirmation, and read-state deletion before confirmation; retries reuse the stable operation ID and never restore deleted product or read-state rows

DELETE thread responses preserve the authenticated AppServer envelope while distinguishing committed HTTP 200, replayable pending HTTP 202, busy HTTP 409, missing HTTP 404, and failed contract HTTP 500 outcomes

Canonical threadstore schema version 3 stores only Redeven thread settings, unadmitted queue state, uploads, routing, and security audit. Schema v2 titles are written to Floret outside SQL transactions before a single v2-to-v3 Redeven transaction rebuilds settings, deduplicates admitted uploads to thread ownership, retains queued ownership, and keeps only strict permission snapshot v2 records. Other kinds and versions are rejected without modification

Published Floret v0.12.0 is the single persistent source of truth for admitted conversation entries and attachments, thread titles, turn and run lifecycle, canonical ordinal, failures, control signals, approvals, todos, projections, tool state, context lifecycle, subagent hierarchy, and opaque provider state; Redeven has no second durable copy

SubAgent HTTP, live, activity, and approval payloads use child thread_id or child_thread_id only; subagent_id plus spawn title and objective aliases are rejected, and task_name is required without role, description, or message guessing

Completed terminal status, output, exit code, and duration come from Floret Activity payloads, while Redeven process reads are available only for running PTYs and raw backend errors remain diagnostic-only

Redeven terminal operation audit records only operation outcome, actor context, process_id, and write input byte count; it does not copy Floret run, tool, status, output, result, error, exit, or duration fields

Redeven consumes github.com/floegence/floret v0.12.0 only from the published module with GOWORK=off verification and no replace, go.work, local sibling path, copied contract, Floret schema access, or Floret internal import

Redeven consumes published Flowersec Go and Core v0.27.0 plus Floe Webapp v0.38.0, uses ConnectArtifact for generic connect entry points, retains explicit Direct and Tunnel APIs, and rejects removed grant helpers and Flowersec internal package imports through the existing dependency contract gate

Control reconnect attempts return whether register completed; every registered attempt resets exponential backoff before the next retry, secure random jitter stays within plus or minus 20 percent and the existing 10-second cap, and cancellation ends the wait without another connection attempt

Flowersec RPC request handlers now start lazily with at most 32 active handlers and a bounded 128-request FIFO queue, so Redeven retains the existing concurrency contract without per-stream worker preallocation

Flower keeps the existing message.block_set and timeline.replaced transports while intentionally breaking unsupported pre-release SubAgent aliases and sourcing thread title, lifecycle, latest turn, and attachments from Floret v0.12.0

Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1, while compatibility_epoch advances to 8 and both minimum versions advance to v0.10.0 so an older Desktop cannot parse LocalUIExposure and an older Runtime cannot provide the network exposure contract

Runtime attach status and the private 0600 Desktop launch/status machine report require local_ui_bridge_url for the independent loopback trusted listener; Local UI health may return public local_ui_url and local_ui_urls for display refresh, while public startup events, health, access status, catalogs, display URLs, preferences, and renderer projections never expose the machine-only bridge endpoint

Native Local Environment windows, health verification, and Desktop Flower requests use the trusted loopback endpoint through a non-persistent direct Electron session; Provider remote and external Local UI traffic retain system proxy policy, while a missing or invalid trusted endpoint fails closed without public URL fallback

Desktop SSH and container Open use the same redeven desktop-bridge placement transport and one runtimePlacementBridgeByTargetID record; SSH Runtime installation and process lifecycle readiness remain separate and no SSH local-forward, public URL fallback, Host rewrite, or cached-failure retry path remains

Gateway, Docker runtime, ReDevPlugin integration, terminal attach/history, startup presentation, and open-source hygiene gates remain required release checks and do not gain alternate compatibility or fallback paths
